architect_hints: [service-grouping] User Authentication Backend, Shopping Cart Backend share lifecycle='transient' (backend) → consider placing in same service
[service-grouping] Product Catalog Backend, Inventory Management Backend, Checkout Backend, Order History Backend share lifecycle='persistent' (backend) → consider placing in same service
[service-grouping] Product Catalog UI, Inventory Management UI, Shopping Cart UI, Checkout UI, Order History UI share lifecycle='persistent' (frontend) → consider placing in same service
[async-trigger] Checkout Backend triggers Order History Backend → consider event/callback or background task instead of sync call
[async-trigger] Checkout Backend triggers Order History UI → consider event/callback or background task instead of sync call
[async-trigger] Checkout UI triggers Checkout Backend → consider event/callback or background task instead of sync call
[async-trigger] Checkout UI triggers Order History Backend → consider event/callback or background task instead of sync call
[async-trigger] Checkout UI triggers Order History UI → consider event/callback or background task instead of sync call
[ai-isolation] Product Catalog UI involve AI/ML → recommend dedicated service (different runtime, token cost, latency)
[deployment-complexity] 3 backend service clusters detected → docker-compose will have multiple services; ensure health checks and startup order

[auth-dependency] Shopping Cart UI, Checkout Backend, Checkout UI (and others) are user-scoped → their services must declare depends_on the service containing User Authentication Backend, Auth UI, Shopping Cart Backend. Auth service must start first.
[storage] Requirements mention persistent storage → confirm in-memory dict is acceptable or add DB entity
[scalability] Requirements mention scale/performance → consider stateless design to allow horizontal scaling
